1.01.name: zynkr-content-writer
1.01.summary: The user provides an abstract philosophical question and answers assistant's prompts.
1.01.author: Peter Tu
1.01.input: The user provides an abstract philosophical question and answers assistant's prompts.
1.01.process: Use single-step Socratic questions, give a score and brief comment, add a short parable if shallow, and guide through ~5 rounds toward clarity.
1.01.output: A score, commentary, optional parable, and the next, more focused philosophical question.

1.02.name: content-idea
1.02.summary: Use this agent when you want to brainstorm, explore, or develop article ideas through guided Socratic dialogue.
1.02.author: Peter Tu
1.02.description:
Use this agent when you want to brainstorm, explore, or develop article ideas through guided Socratic dialogue. This agent acts as a thought partner to help you uncover angles, deepen concepts, and refine your ideas into compelling article premises.

<example>
Context: The user wants to explore a vague topic and turn it into a concrete article idea.
user: "I want to write something about AI and creativity but I'm not sure what angle to take."
assistant: "Let me bring in the Socratic ideation partner to help you explore this topic."
<commentary>
Since the user is trying to ideate and refine an article concept, use the Task tool to launch the content-idea agent to guide them through a Socratic dialogue.
</commentary>
</example>

<example>
Context: The user has a rough article idea and wants to stress-test or deepen it.
user: "I'm thinking about writing an article on why remote work is overrated. Can you help me think through it?"
assistant: "Great starting point. Let me use the Socratic ideation partner to help you pressure-test and develop this idea further."
<commentary>
Since the user wants to develop an existing article idea, use the Task tool to launch the content-idea agent to probe the idea with questions and help refine the core thesis.
</commentary>
</example>

<example>
Context: The user is stuck and has no clear direction but knows they want to write something.
user: "I feel like I have something to say about leadership but I can't quite articulate it."
assistant: "Let me use the Socratic ideation partner to help you surface and articulate what's on your mind."
<commentary>
Since the user has a latent idea they can't fully express, use the Task tool to launch the content-idea agent to use questioning techniques to draw out the core insight.
</commentary>
</example>
1.02.input: User gives article draft (topic + core ideas); templates and style guide are preloaded.
1.02.process: Multi-turn - propose 3 outlines, 3 key points per section, then write paragraphs stepwise with user review, following the style guide.
1.02.output: Clear outline, section bullet lists, and a 1000–1200-word article drafted in the user’s style.

1.03.name: content-fission
1.03.summary: Article fission — extract outline + summary from any popular article, then run 3 rounds of reflective dialogue to surface the writer's own refined take, ending with a new actionable outline ready for style + writing hand
1.03.author: Peter Tu
1.03.description: Article fission — extract outline + summary from any popular article, then run 3 rounds of reflective dialogue to surface the writer's own refined take, ending with a new actionable outline ready for style + writing handoff.
1.03.input: User provides an article draft; a Style Guide exists in the knowledge base.
1.03.process: Check presence of article, verify relevance to Style Guide, then list edit suggestions: original text, proposed revision, and reason. Ensure mobile-friendly style, avoid “不是⋯而是⋯”, and confirm choices with the user.
1.03.output: A numbered list of edit suggestions and a question asking which to adopt.

1.04.name: content-style-select
1.04.summary: Triggered when a user has an article draft (topic + initial thoughts) and needs help selecting a suitable article structure and mapping out section key points.
1.04.author: Peter Tu
1.04.description: Triggered when a user has an article draft (topic + initial thoughts) and needs help selecting a suitable article structure and mapping out section key points. Use this agent when the user says things like "Help me pick a structure for my article", "I have a draft idea, what structure should I use?", "幫我選文章架構", or "我有初稿想法，幫我規劃結構". <example> Context: The user has a topic and some initial thoughts and wants to structure their article. user: "我想寫一篇關於如何利用 AI 提升內容創作效率的文章，有一些初步想法，幫我選一個合適的架構。" assistant: "Let me bring in the content-style-select agent to help you choose the best structure and plan out your section key points." <commentary> Since the user has a draft topic and wants structure guidance, use the Task tool to launch the content-style-select agent. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: The user wants to plan their article before writing. user: "Help me pick a structure for my article about remote work productivity." assistant: "I'll use the content-style-select agent to recommend the most fitting structures for your topic." <commentary> Since the user needs structure selection help, launch the content-style-select agent. </commentary> </example>
1.04.input: User provides an article for critique; limited Memory (SEO + style prefs) may be used.
1.04.process: Evaluate structure, persuasiveness, SEO, and readability; analyze flaws from a third-person, oppositional stance; give scores with reasons; list SEO keywords using allowed Memory; provide directional improvements without rewriting.
1.04.output: Fixed-format: scoring table, opponent-style critique, SEO keyword list with rationale, and non-rewriting improvement suggestions.

1.05.name: content-draft
1.05.summary: Triggered when the user has a confirmed article structure and section key points (typically from the content-style-select handoff summary) and is ready to draft the article paragraph by paragraph.
1.05.author: Peter Tu
1.05.description: Triggered when the user has a confirmed article structure and section key points (typically from the content-style-select handoff summary) and is ready to draft the article paragraph by paragraph. Use this agent when the user says things like "幫我開始撰寫文章", "I have my structure and key points ready, let's start drafting", "開始逐段撰寫", or "可以開始寫了". <example> Context: The user has received a handoff summary from content-style-select and is ready to write. user: "架構和要點都確認了，幫我開始撰寫文章。" assistant: "Let me bring in the content-draft agent to draft the article paragraph by paragraph." <commentary> Since the user has a confirmed structure and key points and wants to start writing, use the Task tool to launch the content-draft agent. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: The user wants to start drafting after planning is complete. user: "I have my structure and key points ready, let's start drafting." assistant: "I'll use the content-draft agent to draft your article section by section." <commentary> Since the user is ready to draft, launch the content-draft agent. </commentary> </example>
1.05.input: User provides an article, topic, or file; limited Memory (SEO + style preferences) may be referenced.
1.05.process: Generate either 10 SEO titles or a full SEO strategy. Use Taiwan search habits, five MECE topic categories, keyword groups, outlines, and optimization advice. Never invent memory or rewrite user content.
1.05.output: SEO titles with reasons and MECE classification, or a complete SEO strategy with keywords, outline, and recommendations.

1.06.name: content-newsletter-draft
1.06.summary: Drafts Peter's weekly Chinese newsletter from the user's article outline or topic idea, shaping it into a structured handoff for the /zynkr-content-writer pipeline.
1.06.author: Peter Tu
1.06.description: Drafts Peter's weekly Chinese newsletter from the user's article outline or topic idea, shaping it into a structured handoff for the /zynkr-content-writer pipeline. Use this skill whenever Peter says "幫我寫電子報", "來寫電子報", "寫newsletter", "電子報大綱", or shares a topic/outline and wants it shaped into a newsletter. Trigger even if Peter only gives a rough idea — the skill will structure it into a full outline.
1.06.input: An article draft provided by the author.
1.06.process: Analyze the article’s theme, audience, and conversion goal, then match it to approved CTA categories and templates defined in Zynkr’s CTA handbook .
1.06.output: Three ready-to-use CTA options with titles, copy, category, sub-goal, placement advice, and validated links.

1.07.name: content-editor
1.07.summary: Triggered when the user has a completed article draft and wants editorial review and improvement suggestions.
1.07.author: Peter Tu
1.07.description: Triggered when the user has a completed article draft and wants editorial review and improvement suggestions. Use this agent when the user says things like "幫我校稿", "review my article draft", "文章寫好了，幫我編輯", "check for edits", or "可以校稿了". <example> Context: The user has a completed draft from content-draft and wants editorial review. user: "初稿完成了，幫我校稿。" assistant: "Let me bring in the content-editor agent to review your draft and provide improvement suggestions." <commentary> Since the user has a completed draft and wants editorial review, use the Task tool to launch the content-editor agent. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: The user wants to check their article for edits. user: "文章寫好了，幫我編輯一下。" assistant: "I'll use the content-editor agent to review your article and suggest edits." <commentary> Since the user wants editorial review of a completed draft, launch the content-editor agent. </commentary> </example>
1.07.process: Stage 3 in the zynkr-content-writer pipeline, orchestrated by zynkr-content-writer.
1.07.output: Polished article (fetches forbidden words, applies style guide edits)

1.08.name: content-reader
1.08.summary: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants a reader's perspective review with scoring and critical analysis.
1.08.author: Peter Tu
1.08.description: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants a reader's perspective review with scoring and critical analysis. Use this agent when the user says things like "幫我審稿", "讀者視角分析", "reader review", "批判性評估", "幫我評分", or "以讀者角度來看". <example> Context: The user has a completed article and wants critical feedback from a reader's perspective. user: "文章寫好了，幫我做讀者審稿。" assistant: "Let me bring in the content-reader agent to analyze your article from a critical reader's viewpoint." <commentary> Since the user wants a reader's perspective review of their article, use the Task tool to launch the content-reader agent. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: The user wants to score and evaluate their article before publishing. user: "幫我從反對者角度審視這篇文章。" assistant: "I'll use the content-reader agent to critically evaluate your article and provide a detailed score." <commentary> Since the user wants critical analysis from an opposition perspective, launch the content-reader agent. </commentary> </example>
1.08.input: A full or partial article text, optionally with a writing or critique goal.
1.08.process: Analyze structure and ideas, extract an outline and summary, reflect through guided questions, then synthesize refined insights.
1.08.output: Clear outlines, summaries, virality analysis, reflective dialogue, and a new actionable outline.

1.09.name: content-title
1.09.summary: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants SEO title suggestions, or when the user provides a topic and wants a full SEO content strategy.
1.09.author: Peter Tu
1.09.description: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants SEO title suggestions, or when the user provides a topic and wants a full SEO content strategy. Use this agent when the user says things like "幫我想標題", "SEO標題建議", "generate titles", "幫我做SEO", "文章標題", "SEO策略", or "幫我規劃關鍵字". <example> Context: The user has a completed article and wants SEO title options. user: "文章寫好了，幫我想10個SEO標題。" assistant: "Let me bring in the content-title agent to generate SEO-optimized titles for your article." <commentary> Since the user has a completed article and wants SEO titles, use the Task tool to launch the content-title agent. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: The user has a topic and wants a full SEO content strategy. user: "我想寫關於外商面試的文章，幫我規劃SEO策略。" assistant: "I'll use the content-title agent to build a full SEO strategy for your topic." <commentary> Since the user wants an SEO strategy for a given topic, launch the content-title agent. </commentary> </example>
1.09.input: User provides text in English or another language.
1.09.process: I translate it into natural, conversational Traditional Chinese using Taiwanese expressions and tone.
1.09.output: Fluent, localized Traditional Chinese (Taiwan) text that sounds like everyday spoken or written language.

1.10.name: content-cta
1.10.summary: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants CTA (Call-To-Action) suggestions.
1.10.author: Peter Tu
1.10.description: Triggered when the user has a completed article and wants CTA (Call-To-Action) suggestions. Use this agent when the user says things like "幫我寫CTA", "CTA建議", "write a CTA", "幫我加CTA", "文章結尾CTA", or "call to action". <example> Context: The user has a completed article and wants CTA options to place at the end. user: "文章寫好了，幫我想三個CTA。" assistant: "Let me bring in the content-cta agent to analyze your article and suggest the best CTAs." <commentary> Since the user has a completed article and wants CTA suggestions, use the Task tool to launch the content-cta agent. </commentary> </example> <example> Context: The user wants a CTA that matches the article's goal. user: "幫我根據這篇文章選合適的CTA。" assistant: "I'll use the content-cta agent to read the article and generate three matched CTA options." <commentary> Since the user wants matched CTA suggestions for their article, launch the content-cta agent. </commentary> </example>
1.10.input: Unstructured user context (intent, knowledge, audience, workflow) plus two reference documents: AI rules (K1) and role distribution (K2).
1.10.process: Validate context → select target role → generate role-specific workplace problems using a strict 3-stage IPO pipeline.
1.10.output: Clear validation result, ranked role options, and 3 realistic workplace problems for AI learning use cases.

1.12.name: content-governance
1.12.summary: Put a newsletter in its proper place — reconcile it between Google Docs and the Notion Content DB in EITHER direction (Doc-first OR Notion-first), and file the Doc into the newsletter Drive folder.
1.12.author: Peter Tu
1.12.description: Put a newsletter in its proper place — reconcile it between Google Docs and the Notion Content DB in EITHER direction (Doc-first OR Notion-first), and file the Doc into the newsletter Drive folder. The goal is that the content ends up correctly placed and cross-linked regardless of which was created first. Use whenever the user shares a Google Doc and/or a Notion Content-DB page and wants them synced, filed, archived, or "put in the right place". Triggers on "幫我放上 Notion", "sync to Notion", "同步到 Notion", "add this to Notion", "幫我歸檔電子報", "file this newsletter", "put this in the right place", "從 Notion 的想法建 Doc", or any message pairing a Doc and/or a Notion page with sync / file / archive intent.
1.12.input: A Google Doc URL and/or a Notion Content-DB page (idea/stub) for a newsletter — in either order
1.12.process: Detect entry point (Doc-first / Notion-first / both), create whichever anchor is missing, file the Doc into the newsletter Drive folder, assign the issue number, classify Subject tags, and sync full metadata + body into a single deduped Notion Content-DB...
1.12.output: A filed Google Doc (newsletter folder, (N) Title) + a deduped Notion Content-DB page with full metadata, cross-linked

1.13.name: content-translator
1.13.summary: Translate English (or any source language) into natural, conversational Traditional Chinese using Taiwanese expressions and tone.
1.13.author: Peter Tu
1.13.description: Translate English (or any source language) into natural, conversational Traditional Chinese using Taiwanese expressions and tone. Removes the 'translation smell' that comes from word-for-word renderings. Also supports a reverse zh-TW → English mode for producing English 'flagship' versions of SEO articles (used by seo-article-pipeline for AEO reach in English-dominant AI engines).
1.13.input: Text in English or another language (plain text, paragraph, document, or code-block-free passage)
1.13.process: Translate into natural conversational Traditional Chinese using Taiwanese expressions and tone; localize idioms, units, and references; preserve any code fences untouched
1.13.output: Fluent, localized Traditional Chinese (Taiwan) text that sounds like everyday spoken or written language, not machine-translated

1.14.name: seo-article-pipeline
1.14.summary: The orchestrator for Zynkr SEO content production: it chains the stages of the v2 flowchart into one pipeline running from brand material all the way to a published article on the live website.
1.14.author: Peter Tu
1.14.description: The orchestrator for Zynkr SEO content production: it chains the stages of the v2 flowchart into one pipeline running from brand material all the way to a published article on the live website. The first half (persona → seed questions → angles → keyword map → intent classification → demand validation → Brief → outline/FAQ) calls each SEO skill; the second half (drafting → titling → proofreading) reuses the existing write-article sub-agents; then it does SEO setup (`seo-article-finalizer` produces a publish-ready Google Doc), publishes to the live website via `seo-publish-article` (which also archives the Doc + updates the SEO tracker), and optionally produces the EN flagship. Triggers when the user says 「跑 SEO 文章流程」, 「開始寫一篇 SEO 文章」, 「/seo-article-pipeline」, or hands over a brand/article material packet.
1.14.input: A brand material packet (first time) or an article material packet (per article); or a SEO_PACKET handoff packet from any stage
1.14.process: Detect the entry point → call the corresponding SEO skill / existing sub-agent stage by stage → a manual gate at every stage → save the working files into this article's Drive subfolder
1.14.output: A published article live at zynkr.ai/blog/<slug> (Doc archived + SEO tracker updated), having passed SEO/AEO proofreading, with meta/schema/links — zh-TW, plus optional EN flagship

1.15.name: seo-persona-builder
1.15.summary: First leg of the SEO content pipeline: reads brand material (from the SEO knowledge-base folder or a brand-material pack) and builds personas / consumer profiles of the target readers and prospects, to serve as the basis
1.15.author: Peter Tu
1.15.description: First leg of the SEO content pipeline: reads brand material (from the SEO knowledge-base folder or a brand-material pack) and builds personas / consumer profiles of the target readers and prospects, to serve as the basis for downstream keyword and topic work. Triggers when the user says '幫我建立 SEO 人物誌', '先做受眾輪廓', '開始 SEO 流程', or hands over a brand-material pack. Does personas only — no keyword work, no article writing.
1.15.input: Brand material (brand culture, website content, industry background, product lineup, best-selling products) — from the SEO knowledge-base Drive folder or a brand-material pack
1.15.process: Read brand material → extract 1–3 prospect personas per the persona-rubric (including pain points, decision-making role, buying journey) → get human review approval → output the handoff packet
1.15.output: 1–3 consumer personas + handoff packet, passed to seo-question-miner for common-question ideation

1.16.name: seo-question-miner
1.16.summary: Second leg of the SEO pipeline: based on the persona, brainstorm the questions your target readers actually ask before buying, then work backward from those questions to seed keywords.
1.16.author: Peter Tu
1.16.description: Second leg of the SEO pipeline: based on the persona, brainstorm the questions your target readers actually ask before buying, then work backward from those questions to seed keywords. Maps to the v2 flowchart step 'brainstorm common questions + interview-answer common questions and list keywords'. Triggers when the user hands over the persona handoff packet or says '發想常見問題' or '挖種子關鍵字'. Only brainstorms questions and seed keywords; it does not do difficulty analysis and does not write articles.
1.16.input: The SEO_PACKET ▸ Persona handoff packet from seo-persona-builder
1.16.process: Following question-frames, expand pre-purchase questions from each persona's pain points (problem / comparison / use case) → work backward from the questions to seed keywords → human review → handoff
1.16.output: A staged (TOFU/MOFU/BOFU) buyer-question list + seed keywords, handed off to seo-angle-finder

1.17.name: seo-angle-finder
1.17.summary: Third leg of the SEO pipeline: working from an SEO expert's perspective plus competitor URLs, surface the content angles and differentiation opportunities for 'where we can do better.' Maps to the v2 flow diagram step 't
1.17.author: Peter Tu
1.17.description: Third leg of the SEO pipeline: working from an SEO expert's perspective plus competitor URLs, surface the content angles and differentiation opportunities for 'where we can do better.' Maps to the v2 flow diagram step 'think about strengthening from the SEO angle + reference competitor URLs to think about strengthening.' Triggers when the user hands over a Questions handoff packet, provides competitor URLs, or says 「找 SEO 切角」. It only finds angles — it does not build the keyword map and does not write the article.
1.17.input: The SEO_PACKET ▸ Questions from seo-question-miner; optional: known competitor URLs (article reference pack)
1.17.process: From the questions and competitor content, surface unmet angles, points to strengthen, and entry points for Zynkr's first-hand evidence → human validation → handoff
1.17.output: Differentiated content angle list (including competitor gaps), handed off to seo-keyword-mapper

1.18.name: seo-keyword-mapper
1.18.summary: Fourth leg of the SEO pipeline: use keyword tools (Ubersuggest / AnswerThePublic) to expand angles and seed terms into a complete keyword map.
1.18.author: Peter Tu
1.18.description: Fourth leg of the SEO pipeline: use keyword tools (Ubersuggest / AnswerThePublic) to expand angles and seed terms into a complete keyword map. Maps to the v2 flow diagram's 'filter keywords to build the keyword map + compile the complete keyword map'. Triggers when the user hands over an Angles handoff packet, pastes a keyword-tool export, or says '做關鍵字地圖'. Builds the map only — it does not classify intent and does not write articles.
1.18.input: seo-angle-finder's SEO_PACKET ▸ Angles; a Ubersuggest / AnswerThePublic export pasted by the user (CSV/text)
1.18.process: Expand seed terms per keyword-checklist → compile a keyword map with search volume/difficulty (thread head → keyword) → human supplements via tools → hand off
1.18.output: A complete keyword map (keywords, thread heads, volume/difficulty), handed to seo-keyword-classifier

1.19.name: seo-keyword-classifier
1.19.summary: Fifth leg of the SEO pipeline: classify the keyword map "by search intent" (not by product category), and offer several grouping versions to choose from.
1.19.author: Peter Tu
1.19.description: Fifth leg of the SEO pipeline: classify the keyword map "by search intent" (not by product category), and offer several grouping versions to choose from. Maps to the v2 flowchart step "classify by different classification methods, provide different versions". Triggers when the user hands over a KeywordMap handoff packet, or says '關鍵字分類' or '按意圖分群'. Only classifies — does not validate demand or difficulty, and does not write articles.
1.19.process: Group by search intent per intent-taxonomy + apply the keyword-sop screening principles → produce 2–3 grouping versions → human Trigger picks a version → hand off
1.19.output: Keywords grouped by intent (pick one of multiple versions), handed off to seo-demand-validator

1.20.name: seo-demand-validator
1.20.summary: Sixth stage of the SEO pipeline: validate the real demand and competitive difficulty of keywords, compare the common patterns across the current SERP, prune unnecessary terms, and consolidate a list of target keywords an
1.20.author: Peter Tu
1.20.description: Sixth stage of the SEO pipeline: validate the real demand and competitive difficulty of keywords, compare the common patterns across the current SERP, prune unnecessary terms, and consolidate a list of target keywords and topics. Corresponds to node (6) in the v2 flow diagram. Triggers when the user hands over a Classified handoff packet or says 「驗證需求」 (validate demand) or 「整理主題清單」 (organize the topic list). It only validates and converges — it does not write the Brief and does not write the article.
1.20.input: seo-keyword-classifier's SEO_PACKET ▸ Classified; manual Google/SERP observations and tool-based difficulty data
1.20.process: Compare SERP common patterns and competitive difficulty per the competitor-review-table → manually validate demand, prune unnecessary terms → converge into a topic list + target keywords → hand off
1.20.output: Topic list + target keywords + SERP common patterns, handed off to seo-brief-writer

1.21.name: seo-brief-writer
1.21.summary: Seventh stage of the SEO pipeline: for one selected topic, find the value proposition and winning angle, and produce a complete SEO writing Brief.
1.21.author: Peter Tu
1.21.description: Seventh stage of the SEO pipeline: for one selected topic, find the value proposition and winning angle, and produce a complete SEO writing Brief. Corresponds to the v2 flowchart step 「選擇主題：設計 Brief」. Triggers when the user hands over a Topics handoff packet, provides reference articles/forums/expert opinions, or says 「寫 Brief」. Only writes the Brief — does not design the outline and does not write the article.
1.21.input: The SEO_PACKET ▸ Topics from seo-demand-validator; optional: reference articles / forum Q&A / expert opinions (an article reference pack)
1.21.process: Analyze ceiling articles per ceiling-article-features → find the winning angle and value proposition per winning-key-scoring → apply brief-template to produce the Brief → human verification and augmentation → hand off
1.21.output: One SEO writing Brief, handed to seo-outline-designer

1.22.name: seo-outline-designer
1.22.summary: Stage 8 of the SEO pipeline: turn the SEO Brief into an article outline and FAQ section, outputting a handoff summary compatible with content-draft.
1.22.author: Peter Tu
1.22.description: Stage 8 of the SEO pipeline: turn the SEO Brief into an article outline and FAQ section, outputting a handoff summary compatible with content-draft. Corresponds to the v2 flow diagram's 'Structure article: outline design' and 'Build FAQ'. Triggers when the user hands over a Brief handoff packet and says '設計大綱' or '做 FAQ'. Only handles the outline and FAQ; the actual writing is handed off to content-draft.
1.22.input: seo-brief-writer's SEO_PACKET ▸ Brief; optional: whether to supplement information (other reference articles), client first-hand information
1.22.process: Design the H2/H3 outline per outline-patterns (answer-first opening) → build the FAQ from the Brief's must-answer questions → decide whether to supplement information → human review → output an content-draft-compatible handoff summary
1.22.output: Article outline + FAQ + handoff summary of target keywords, handed off to content-draft (1.06) for writing

1.23.name: seo-article-finalizer
1.23.summary: The final leg of the SEO pipeline: add internal/backlinks, a meta description, and structured data (schema) to an already-proofed article, then assemble it into a publish-ready Google Doc (article body + a 「SEO 交付物」block
1.23.author: Peter Tu
1.23.description: The final leg of the SEO pipeline: add internal/backlinks, a meta description, and structured data (schema) to an already-proofed article, then assemble it into a publish-ready Google Doc (article body + a 「SEO 交付物」block carrying slug/seo_title/meta_description/keywords/category + links/schema) for seo-publish-article. Corresponds to node (11) of the v2 flow diagram — 'list which paragraphs can take links + add the meta description' and 'article settings'. Triggers when the user hands over an already-proofed article and says '做 SEO 設定', '上 meta', '準備上架', or '產出可上架的 Google Doc'. Does pre-publish settings + assembles the Doc; does not rewrite content, and does not publish/move/track (that's seo-publish-article).
1.23.input: An article proofed by content-editor (including the SEO review rubric) + that article's SEO_PACKET ▸ Brief
1.23.process: Mark internal/backlink paragraphs → write the meta description + FAQ/Article schema → human confirm → assemble the article + settings into a publish-ready Google Doc (working folder) handed to seo-publish-article
1.23.output: A publish-ready Google Doc (article body + 「SEO 交付物」block: slug/seo_title/meta/keywords/category + links/schema) in the SEO working folder, handed to seo-publish-article

1.24.name: zynkr-slide
1.24.summary: The conductor of the slide relay (sitting above the three-stage relay): fires when the user says '做一份簡報／做個 deck／幫我把這些做成投影片／做季報簡報／all-hands 簡報／deep dive 簡報／pitch deck'.
1.24.author: Peter Tu
1.24.description: The conductor of the slide relay (sitting above the three-stage relay): fires when the user says '做一份簡報／做個 deck／幫我把這些做成投影片／做季報簡報／all-hands 簡報／deep dive 簡報／pitch deck'. 6.02.name: zynkr

6.02.author: Peter Tu
6.02.description: The front-door router for the Zynkr assistant ecosystem. Takes ANY input from the user — a URL, a file path, a slug, a vague idea, a question about pipeline state — figures out which Zynkr skill it belongs to, reads the relevant state across GitHub Project / issues / on-disk / live marketplace, and either auto-invokes the right sub-skill or asks one targeted clarifying question. Use this skill whenever the user drops something into the conversation without specifying which skill should handle it — every undirected URL, file mention, idea, status question, or 'what should I do with this?' is a candidate. Especially fire on phrases like 'I found this', 'I just built X', 'where am I with Y', 'what's in my queue', 'I have an idea about', '幫我看一下這個', '/zynkr', or when the user pastes a link with no further direction. Triggers MORE eagerly than the specific skills below it — when in doubt, route through /zynkr rather than asking the user to remember the right slash command. Also fire on deck / 簡報 requests — 'make me a deck', '幫我做簡報', '做投影片', 'pitch deck', 'deep dive 簡報', 'all-hands 簡報', '把這份資料做成簡報' — and route them to /zynkr-slide (the slide-relay orchestrator).
6.02.input: Anything: a URL, local file path, skill slug, free-text idea, status question, or pasted content. The skill classifies the input shape before doing anything else.
6.02.process: Classify input shape → look up state across the four signals (Project / issues / on-disk / live API) when the input references a skill-pipeline item → route to the right sub-skill via the Skill tool when confidence is high, or ask one targeted clarifying...
6.02.output: Either: (a) an auto-invocation of the right Zynkr sub-skill (most common); (b) one targeted clarifying question when intent is genuinely ambiguous; (c) a compact state table...

1.29.description: Publishes a finalized post to your Facebook, Threads & Instagram channels via the Buffer API — per-channel formatting, Instagram image support, and a preview-confirm gate before anything posts. Use when you say 'publish my post', 'post this to Threads/IG', 'push this draft live', or 'schedule this post'.
1.29.input: A finalized post/caption (plus a public image URL for Instagram), which channels to target, and whether to draft, post now, or schedule
1.29.process: Formats the copy per channel (Threads 500-char aware; Instagram needs a public image URL + caption), previews the exact copy for confirmation, then publishes through the Buffer API using your own token and connected channels
1.29.output: The post created in Buffer — as a draft, an immediate publish, or a scheduled post — with the Buffer post id and status returned per channel

1.30.name: seo-publish-article
1.30.summary: Publishes a FINISHED SEO blog article to the live website (e.g.
1.30.author: Peter Tu
1.30.description: Publishes a FINISHED SEO blog article to the live website (e.g. zynkr.ai/blog) by writing it straight into the CMS's Supabase `articles` table — the fast path, because the CMS admin page (e.g. cms.zynkr.ai/content) is an auth-gated dashboard, not a write API. Reads the article from a Google Doc (body + the embedded 「SEO 交付物」block for slug/seo_title/meta_description/keywords/category), converts it to CMS-safe Tiptap JSON + content_html (tables→lists, ▋ headings, schema-validated so it never corrupts on a later in-CMS edit), shows a preview, and on your explicit confirm publishes it live (status=published + published_at) then verifies the public URL renders. Use whenever the user already has a finished/proofed article and says 上架文章 / 發佈到網站 / 把這篇放到部落格 / 上線這篇文章 / publish this article to the blog / push this post live on the site / put this on the web — or hands over a finished article Doc and wants it on the web. Boundaries — this is the WEB/CMS publisher and should win against, but NOT be confused with: /publish-article (posts to SOCIAL channels like Threads/IG/FB via Buffer), /seo-article-finalizer (only adds meta/links/schema, does NOT publish), article DRAFTING (the SEO pipeline / zynkr-content-writer write the article), and /skill-publish (ships a SKILL to the marketplace, not an article). Trigger eagerly whenever a finished, proofed article needs to go live on the website.
1.30.input: A finished SEO article (Google Doc or markdown) with its 「SEO 交付物」metadata block, plus the local CMS repo for its Supabase URL + service-role key.
1.30.process: Read Doc → split body/metadata → build + validate Tiptap JSON + content_html → resolve ids + slug check → preview → confirm → POST to Supabase `articles` (service role, published) → verify /blog/<slug> → archive Doc + update tracker
1.30.output: The article live at <site>/blog/<slug> — inserted articles row + verified page; the source Doc archived and the SEO tracker (Topic List + Keyword Pool) updated.

3.14.name: accupass-agent
3.14.summary: Guides the operator through preparing a complete Accupass event listing — one field at a time — then outputs a single copy-paste-ready template covering event content, ticketing, marketing emails, and a post-event follow
3.14.author: Jane Liao
3.14.description: Guides the operator through preparing a complete Accupass event listing — one field at a time — then outputs a single copy-paste-ready template covering event content, ticketing, marketing emails, and a post-event follow-up checklist. Use when the user says '我要上架活動', '上架活動通', '填活動通', '活動上架', 'accupass', '幫我填模板', or '活動資訊填寫'. Does not publish to Accupass directly (the platform has no API) — it produces the filled template for manual entry.
3.14.input: Event seed material (existing event description, outline, or a past event listing) plus the event format (online / in-person / hybrid)
3.14.process: Two interview phases — event content fields confirmed one at a time, then ticketing & schedule fields — followed by one consolidated template output
3.14.output: A fully filled Accupass listing template: pre-launch goals, platform settings, event copy, marketing emails, reminder email, and post-event follow-up checklist

3.15.name: guest-lecturer-program
3.15.summary: Manages the full external guest-lecturer collaboration lifecycle for Zynkr events — lecturer intake and screening, contract preparation, the per-event D-30→D+3 operations timeline, and monthly fee settlement with statuto
3.15.author: Jane Liao
3.15.description: Manages the full external guest-lecturer collaboration lifecycle for Zynkr events — lecturer intake and screening, contract preparation, the per-event D-30→D+3 operations timeline, and monthly fee settlement with statutory tax withholding. Use when the user says '外部講師', '講師合作', '講師結算', '講師合約', 'guest lecturer', or asks to run any stage of the lecturer program (接洽新講師 / 上架講師活動 / 月末結算). Commercial terms (revenue split, thresholds, penalty caps) come from a local program-config.md — they are never hardcoded in this skill.
3.15.input: The lifecycle stage to run (intake / contract / event ops / settlement) plus the lecturer or event in question; commercial parameters from a local program-config.md
3.15.process: Stage-based playbook: screen and onboard lecturers via form + tracker, generate contracts from templates, drive the D-30→D+3 per-event SOP checklist, then compute monthly settlement (fees → net revenue → split → withholding) and issue the statement
3.15.output: Stage artifacts: lecturer tracker updates, contract drafts, per-event checklist status, and a monthly settlement statement ready for lecturer sign-off and finance payout
